top_issues: The desktop home hero is still slightly more theatrical than a perfect conversion-first hierarchy wants.
The sample route could still lose a little more below-the-fold explanation now that the first-step form wins the page immediately.
The builder's deepest success, retry, and failure states were not all re-audited as rendered end-to-end screens in this pass.
The system is now premium in rest states and validation states, but not yet fully proven across every terminal workflow state.
top_strengths: Home and sample now expose primary actions in the first mobile viewport.
The sample route now behaves like a genuine first-step acquisition screen on both desktop and mobile.
The create flow now shows step context and both format choices immediately across breakpoints.
The brand visual system remains distinctive, warm, trustworthy, and now supports the funnel instead of competing with it.
recommended_focus: Run one final lifecycle-state audit through upload completion, order review, and recoverable failure screens.
Reduce the desktop home hero one more step if the goal is a literal 10.0 conversion score.
Compress one more lower section on /sample so the route becomes even more operational after the first viewport.
Keep the current state language as the standard for every later builder step.
